infrastructure.name: .dockerignore

infrastructure.name: ci.yml
infrastructure.summary: Main CI workflow triggered on pull_request and push to main, running four parallel jobs: secret scanning with TruffleHog and Gitleaks, TypeScript type checking, workspace guard tests, and a DB type-drift guard that spins up a local Supabase instance to detect stale generated types.

infrastructure.name: secret-scan
infrastructure.summary: Scans every commit for leaked credentials using TruffleHog (verified secrets only) and Gitleaks on full git history.

infrastructure.name: check
infrastructure.summary: Installs pnpm dependencies and runs the monorepo-wide TypeScript type-check via `pnpm run check`.

infrastructure.name: workspace-guards
infrastructure.summary: Runs workspace guard tests (`pnpm run workspace:test`) to enforce cross-package invariants.

infrastructure.name: type-drift
infrastructure.summary: Starts a local Supabase instance, regenerates TypeScript database types, and fails if the committed `database.types.ts` is stale compared to the live schema.

infrastructure.name: publish-packages.yml
infrastructure.summary: Publishes the `packages/contracts` package to GitHub Packages (npm registry) when changes are pushed to main under `packages/contracts/src/**` or `.changeset/**`; skips publication idempotently if the version is already published.

infrastructure.name: publish
infrastructure.summary: Checks if the current package version is already published to GitHub Packages; if not, runs `pnpm publish` for the contracts package.

infrastructure.name: railway-cron-drift.yml
infrastructure.summary: Guards against Railway cron schedule drift on pull requests by running Terraform plan against the `infra/railway-cron-drift` config and failing if the live Railway environment diverges from the declared state.

infrastructure.name: drift-guard
infrastructure.summary: Prints Railway cron diagnostics, validates Railway API token, runs `terraform init` and `validate`, then executes `terraform plan` with `--detailed-exitcode` to detect any drift.

infrastructure.name: railway-sandbox-verify.yml
infrastructure.summary: Conditionally runs an arbitrary command inside a Railway sandbox environment for PRs labeled `railway-sandbox` or touching Railway-related files, plus supports manual dispatch with configurable command and private-network options.

infrastructure.name: evaluate_trigger
infrastructure.summary: Evaluates whether the sandbox verification job should run by checking for the `railway-sandbox` PR label or detecting changes to Railway-related paths.

infrastructure.name: sandbox-verify
infrastructure.summary: Installs dependencies and runs the Railway sandbox repo-check script with the configured command, ref, and optional private-network flag.

infrastructure.name: main.tf
infrastructure.summary: Terraform module implementing Railway cron drift detection: reads cron-services.json as the expected manifest, optionally fetches live Railway service metadata via GraphQL, computes field-level diagnostics for each cron service, and enforces correctness via output preconditions used by GitHub Actions and Spacelift.

infrastructure.name: versions.tf
infrastructure.summary: Declares Terraform version constraint (>=1.5.0) and pins the hashicorp/http provider (~>3.5) used by main.tf for Railway GraphQL calls.

data.name: 20260601000000_schema_baseline.sql
data.summary: Consolidated schema baseline consolidating all prior migrations; defines 23 core tables (events, cities, tags, event_sources, user_profiles, invite system, source/tag queues, audit logs, etc.) and 40+ private/public RPC functions including bulk_import_scrape_events, admin CRUD helpers, and search RPCs.

data.name: source_scrape_queue
data.summary: Queue table tracking source scraping jobs with status, attempt counts, and retry timing.

data.name: event_tag_queue
data.summary: Queue table tracking AI tagging jobs for individual events with status and retry tracking.

data.name: cities
data.summary: Reference table for supported cities with geographic coordinates, slug, and timezone.

data.name: event_sources

data.name: tags

data.name: user_profiles

data.name: invite_codes

data.name: bulk_import_scrape_events
data.summary: SECURITY DEFINER RPC that batch-imports scraped events via a single CTE chain, classifying each as insert/update, applying admin_locked_fields, and bulk-enqueuing tag jobs. Returns imported/updated/skipped/enqueued counts.

data.summary: pgTAP-style SQL test verifying that public.admin_db_health_snapshot() is blocked for anon and non-admin roles, returns a valid JSONB object for enabled admins, and contains the expected keys including tag_queue_by_status and snapshot_at.

data.name: admin_events_rpc.sql
data.summary: Comprehensive SQL test for admin event RPCs (admin_events_enriched, admin_event_facets) verifying that non-admin, anon, and expired-admin callers are denied access while active admins can call these functions and receive correctly shaped results.

data.name: api_surface_hardening.sql
data.summary: Minimal SQL guard test asserting that pg_graphql is not installed and that the anon role holds no INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, or other mutation grants on any public schema table.

data.name: cron_rpc_security.sql
data.summary: SQL test verifying that cron management RPCs (admin_list_railway_cron_jobs and related write functions) are restricted to enabled admins for reads and service_role for writes, blocking non-admin authenticated users and anon.

data.name: cron_toggle.sql
data.summary: SQL test verifying the cron enable/disable lifecycle: anon cannot call is_cron_enabled, service_role can, non-admin authenticated users cannot call admin_set_cron_enabled, and enabled admins can toggle a cron label off and back on.

data.name: events_cursor_pagination.sql
data.summary: SQL test covering seven cursor-pagination scenarios for events_enriched and search_events RPCs, including first/second page correctness, city filter, date range filter, same-datetime tie-breaking by UUID, anon path returning false for personalization fields, and keyword search.

data.name: events_enriched_parity.sql
data.summary: SQL test asserting behavioral parity of the events_enriched RPC across anon and authenticated paths, covering is_favorited/is_in_calendar flags, zero-rating vs rated aggregates, alphabetically ordered tags JSONB array, draft event exclusion, and city filter scoping.

data.name: invite_gate_oauth_signup.sql
data.summary: SQL test verifying the closed-beta invite gate blocks uninvited Google/Apple OAuth signups when app.settings.require_invite is enabled, while allowing pre-invited email addresses and passing through when the gate is disabled.

data.name: llm_event_review_processing.sql
data.summary: SQL test exercising the LLM event review pipeline: verifies default processing_mode is manual_review, tests manual/auto/LLM source processing modes, and validates that admin_events_enriched filters correctly by llm_review_status and llm_review_decision.

data.name: reference_data.sql
data.summary: SQL smoke test verifying that the production-reset migration baseline includes the expected seed event sources (BREC Parks, Louisiana Philharmonic, Macaroni Kid) and that removed legacy sources and required maintenance RPCs and cron jobs are present or absent as intended.

data.name: rls_access_expiry.sql
data.summary: SQL test verifying that access_expires_at is honored by is_enabled_user() and is_admin() helpers, blocking both regular users and admins whose access has expired even when is_enabled is true.

data.name: rls_anon_read.sql
data.summary: SQL test verifying the anonymous read boundary: anon can see published events via public_events view, can read published raw events rows, is blocked from draft events, can see active cities, and cannot see inactive cities.

data.name: rls_privilege_escalation.sql
data.summary: SQL test verifying three independent layers of defense against user_profiles.role privilege escalation: REVOKE on the role column, a trigger that blocks self-promotion, and the RLS WITH CHECK policy.

data.name: rls_public_events_view.sql
data.summary: SQL integration test for the RLS security boundary around public event previews, verifying anon visibility of published content, child-table (event_tags, ratings, comments) inheritance of the public_events boundary, and plan_events_for_user RPC user-ID enforcement.

data.name: role_smoke.sql
data.summary: Broad role smoke test covering 14 assertions across anon, authenticated, and service_role; verifies correct SELECT grants on public views and tables, correct RPC accessibility (events_enriched, search_events, is_cron_enabled), and that anon is blocked from cron RPCs.

data.name: trace_retention.sql
data.summary: SQL test verifying that private.run_daily_maintenance() prunes rows older than 90 days from event_ai_traces and source_extraction_traces while leaving recent rows intact.

pipeline:.github/workflows/ci.yml.name: ci.yml
pipeline:.github/workflows/ci.yml.summary: Main CI workflow triggered on pull_request and push to main, running four parallel jobs: secret scanning with TruffleHog and Gitleaks, TypeScript type checking, workspace guard tests, and a DB type-drift guard that spins up a local Supabase instance to detect stale generated types.

step:.github/workflows/ci.yml:secret-sca.name: secret-scan
step:.github/workflows/ci.yml:secret-sca.summary: Scans every commit for leaked credentials using TruffleHog (verified secrets only) and Gitleaks on full git history.

step:.github/workflows/ci.yml:check.name: check
step:.github/workflows/ci.yml:check.summary: Installs pnpm dependencies and runs the monorepo-wide TypeScript type-check via `pnpm run check`.

step:.github/workflows/ci.yml:workspace-.name: workspace-guards
step:.github/workflows/ci.yml:workspace-.summary: Runs workspace guard tests (`pnpm run workspace:test`) to enforce cross-package invariants.

step:.github/workflows/ci.yml:type-drift.name: type-drift
step:.github/workflows/ci.yml:type-drift.summary: Starts a local Supabase instance, regenerates TypeScript database types, and fails if the committed `database.types.ts` is stale compared to the live schema.

pipeline:.github/workflows/publish-packa.name: publish-packages.yml
pipeline:.github/workflows/publish-packa.summary: Publishes the `packages/contracts` package to GitHub Packages (npm registry) when changes are pushed to main under `packages/contracts/src/**` or `.changeset/**`; skips publication idempotently if the version is already published.

step:.github/workflows/publish-packages..name: publish
step:.github/workflows/publish-packages..summary: Checks if the current package version is already published to GitHub Packages; if not, runs `pnpm publish` for the contracts package.

pipeline:.github/workflows/railway-cron-.name: railway-cron-drift.yml
pipeline:.github/workflows/railway-cron-.summary: Guards against Railway cron schedule drift on pull requests by running Terraform plan against the `infra/railway-cron-drift` config and failing if the live Railway environment diverges from the declared state.

step:.github/workflows/railway-cron-drif.name: drift-guard
step:.github/workflows/railway-cron-drif.summary: Prints Railway cron diagnostics, validates Railway API token, runs `terraform init` and `validate`, then executes `terraform plan` with `--detailed-exitcode` to detect any drift.

pipeline:.github/workflows/railway-sandb.name: railway-sandbox-verify.yml
pipeline:.github/workflows/railway-sandb.summary: Conditionally runs an arbitrary command inside a Railway sandbox environment for PRs labeled `railway-sandbox` or touching Railway-related files, plus supports manual dispatch with configurable command and private-network options.

step:.github/workflows/railway-sandbox-v.name: evaluate_trigger
step:.github/workflows/railway-sandbox-v.summary: Evaluates whether the sandbox verification job should run by checking for the `railway-sandbox` PR label or detecting changes to Railway-related paths.

step:.github/workflows/railway-sandbox-v.name: sandbox-verify
step:.github/workflows/railway-sandbox-v.summary: Installs dependencies and runs the Railway sandbox repo-check script with the configured command, ref, and optional private-network flag.

config:.env.example.name: .env.example
config:.env.example.summary: Template listing all required environment variables for the monorepo: Supabase connection (URL, anon key, service role key), Resend email API key, Railway deployment token, and GitHub Packages PAT for consuming scoped npm packages.

config:.gitleaks.toml.name: .gitleaks.toml
config:.gitleaks.toml.summary: Gitleaks secret-scanning configuration that extends the default ruleset and defines allowlisted paths (e2e fixtures, SQL test files, iOS project files, lockfiles) and regex patterns (hash placeholders, Supabase publishable keys) to suppress known false positives.

config:package.json.name: package.json
config:package.json.summary: Root pnpm monorepo package manifest declaring workspace-level scripts for changesets, Railway deployment, Supabase database operations (migrate, type generation, advisors), edge-function serving, and testing; devDependencies include TypeScript, oxlint, vitest, changeset tooling, and the Railway CLI.

config:pnpm-workspace.yaml.name: pnpm-workspace.yaml
config:pnpm-workspace.yaml.summary: pnpm workspace definition declaring the packages/* and supabase/functions directories as workspace members, allowing esbuild native builds while suppressing supabase CLI postinstall, and constraining built-dependency installation to esbuild only.

config:graphify-out/.graphify_labels.jso.name: .graphify_labels.json
config:graphify-out/.graphify_labels.jso.summary: Graphify community label index mapping numeric community IDs (0–142) to their string labels, used by the graphify analysis tool to annotate the knowledge graph output.

document:graphify-out/GRAPH_REPORT.md.name: GRAPH_REPORT.md
document:graphify-out/GRAPH_REPORT.md.summary: Comprehensive graphify analysis report for the family-events-backend codebase covering 1774 nodes, 3154 edges, and 143 detected communities with community hubs, god nodes, surprising connections, and knowledge gaps as of 2026-06-12.

config:graphify-out/cost.json.name: cost.json
config:graphify-out/cost.json.summary: Graphify run cost tracking file recording token usage and file counts per analysis run; shows one run on 2026-06-12 that processed 381 files with zero LLM token cost (pure structural extraction).

file:graphify-out/graph.html.name: graph.html
file:graphify-out/graph.html.summary: Interactive HTML visualization of the graphify knowledge graph, rendering the project's node-edge graph in a browser using embedded JavaScript and the graph.json data source.

config:graphify-out/graph.json.name: graph.json
config:graphify-out/graph.json.summary: Full graphify knowledge graph data file containing 1774 nodes, 3154 directed edges, hyperedges, and community assignments for the entire family-events-backend codebase, produced by graphify v0.8.38.

config:graphify-out/manifest.json.name: manifest.json
config:graphify-out/manifest.json.summary: Graphify file manifest cataloging all 381 source files analyzed in the family-events-backend codebase with their absolute paths, used by the graphify tool to track corpus membership and incremental analysis.

config:infra/railway-cron-drift/cron-ser.name: cron-services.json
config:infra/railway-cron-drift/cron-ser.summary: Manifest of 8 Railway cron service definitions (cron-tag-queue, cron-scrape-sources, cron-db-maintenance, cron-cleanup-stale, cron-enrich-events, cron-weekly-digest, cron-send-reminders, cron-review-events), each specifying builder, cron schedule, restart policy, and required deployment status used by main.tf for drift validation.

resource:infra/railway-cron-drift/main.t.name: main.tf
resource:infra/railway-cron-drift/main.t.summary: Terraform module implementing Railway cron drift detection: reads cron-services.json as the expected manifest, optionally fetches live Railway service metadata via GraphQL, computes field-level diagnostics for each cron service, and enforces correctness via output preconditions used by GitHub Actions and Spacelift.

resource:infra/railway-cron-drift/versio.name: versions.tf
resource:infra/railway-cron-drift/versio.summary: Declares Terraform version constraint (>=1.5.0) and pins the hashicorp/http provider (~>3.5) used by main.tf for Railway GraphQL calls.

config:packages/config-quality/oxfmt.bas.name: oxfmt.base.json
config:packages/config-quality/oxfmt.bas.summary: Base oxfmt (Biome-compatible formatter) configuration for the monorepo, specifying 2-space indentation, no semicolons, double quotes, ES5 trailing commas, and 100-character print width.

config:packages/config-quality/oxlint.ba.name: oxlint.base.json
config:packages/config-quality/oxlint.ba.summary: Base oxlint configuration enabling React, TypeScript, unicorn, and oxc plugins with correctness rules as errors and warnings for exhaustive deps, unused vars, and explicit any.

config:packages/config-quality/package.j.name: package.json
config:packages/config-quality/package.j.summary: Package manifest for the shared code-quality config package (@cypress-ink-labs/config-quality), exporting oxfmt and oxlint base configs via the exports map.

config:packages/config-typescript/base.j.name: base.json
config:packages/config-typescript/base.j.summary: Base TypeScript compiler configuration for the monorepo enabling strict mode, ESNext modules with bundler resolution, verbatimModuleSyntax, noEmit, and strict unused-variable checking targeting ES2023.

config:packages/config-typescript/node.j.name: node.json
config:packages/config-typescript/node.j.summary: Node.js-specific TypeScript configuration extending the base config, adding ES2023 lib and @types/node type definitions for server-side packages.

config:packages/config-typescript/packag.name: package.json
config:packages/config-typescript/packag.summary: Package manifest for the shared TypeScript configuration package (@cypress-ink-labs/config-typescript), exporting base, node, and react-vite tsconfig presets via the exports map.

config:packages/config-typescript/react-.name: react-vite.json
config:packages/config-typescript/react-.summary: React + Vite TypeScript configuration extending the base config, adding DOM libs, Vite client types, and react-jsx transform for frontend packages.

document:packages/contracts/CHANGELOG.md.name: CHANGELOG.md
document:packages/contracts/CHANGELOG.md.summary: Release changelog for the @family-events/contracts package, currently tracking version 0.0.2 with patch changes.

config:packages/contracts/package.json.name: package.json
config:packages/contracts/package.json.summary: Package manifest for @cypress-ink-labs/contracts v0.0.3, a published npm package exporting TypeScript types generated from the family-events Supabase schema with GitHub Package Registry publishing config.

config:packages/contracts/tsconfig.json.name: tsconfig.json
config:packages/contracts/tsconfig.json.summary: TypeScript configuration for the contracts package, extending the shared node preset and including all src files.

file:scripts/check-railway.sh.name: check-railway.sh
file:scripts/check-railway.sh.summary: Audits Railway services against repo IaC state via the Railway GraphQL API, detecting rootDirectory and cronSchedule drift; supports a --fix flag to auto-correct mismatches.

function:scripts/check-railway.sh:graphq.name: graphql
function:scripts/check-railway.sh:graphq.summary: Sends a GraphQL query to the Railway API using curl with the stored bearer token.

function:scripts/check-railway.sh:expect.name: expected_root_for
function:scripts/check-railway.sh:expect.summary: Returns the expected rootDirectory for a named Railway service by querying the IaC JSON, or UNMANAGED/NULL if not found.

function:scripts/check-railway.sh:expect.name: expected_cron_for
function:scripts/check-railway.sh:expect.summary: Returns the expected cronSchedule for a named Railway service from IaC JSON, or empty string if not found.

file:scripts/deno-install-functions.sh.name: deno-install-functions.sh
file:scripts/deno-install-functions.sh.summary: Iterates all Supabase Edge Function directories and runs deno install for each function that has both deno.json and index.ts, updating deno.lock files.

file:scripts/deploy.sh.name: deploy.sh
file:scripts/deploy.sh.summary: Thin wrapper around the @cypress-ink-labs/deploy-cli that normalizes argument conventions (adding 'deploy' subcommand when missing) before delegating to pnpm.

file:scripts/setup-local.sh.name: setup-local.sh
file:scripts/setup-local.sh.summary: Re-applies local development settings after a supabase db reset: configures app.settings.* database variables, writes .env.development.local files for the monorepo root and apps/web, and bootstraps the local admin user.

file:scripts/supabase-functions-serve.sh.name: supabase-functions-serve.sh
file:scripts/supabase-functions-serve.sh.summary: Cleans stale Deno node_modules symlinks in the Supabase functions directory before delegating to supabase.sh to start the local functions server.

file:scripts/supabase.sh.name: supabase.sh
file:scripts/supabase.sh.summary: Resolves and invokes the Supabase CLI from the system PATH, the local node_modules binary, or falls back to npx, ensuring consistent CLI behavior across environments.

file:scripts/sync-cron-runner.sh.name: sync-cron-runner.sh
file:scripts/sync-cron-runner.sh.summary: Copies the canonical cron/_shared/cron-runner.sh into each of the eight Railway cron service directories, keeping cron runner scripts in sync after edits to the shared source.

file:scripts/test.sh.name: test.sh
file:scripts/test.sh.summary: Runs the full suite of 15 Supabase SQL integration tests (RLS, RPC, pagination, security, cron) against a target Postgres database resolved from args, env, or local supabase status.

function:scripts/test.sh:usage.name: usage
function:scripts/test.sh:usage.summary: Prints usage instructions for test.sh including DB_URL resolution order and prerequisites.

document:scripts/db/README.md.name: README.md
document:scripts/db/README.md.summary: Documentation for the database evidence and diagnostic scripts directory, describing the purpose and sections of collect-db-evidence.sql and prerequisites for running it.

file:scripts/db/collect-db-evidence.sql.name: collect-db-evidence.sql
file:scripts/db/collect-db-evidence.sql.summary: Read-only production diagnostic SQL script covering pg_stat_statements query performance, index usage, table bloat, constraint pre-validation checks for several tables, and commented EXPLAIN templates for key RPCs.

file:scripts/db/find-missing-end-times.s.name: find-missing-end-times.sql
file:scripts/db/find-missing-end-times.s.summary: Diagnostic SQL script that reports summary statistics and a detailed list of events missing an end_datetime but having a start_datetime, ordered by recency.

file:scripts/db/wipe-live-events.sh.name: wipe-live-events.sh
file:scripts/db/wipe-live-events.sh.summary: Destructive maintenance script that truncates all live event and scrape-history tables from the linked Supabase project and resets event_source health fields, with an interactive confirmation guard unless --yes is passed.

function:scripts/db/wipe-live-events.sh:.name: usage
function:scripts/db/wipe-live-events.sh:.summary: Prints detailed usage instructions for wipe-live-events.sh including all affected tables and available options.

document:supabase/docs/EMAIL.md.name: EMAIL.md
document:supabase/docs/EMAIL.md.summary: Comprehensive setup guide for transactional email delivery via Resend, covering Supabase Auth email wiring, application email configuration, soft-failure behavior, delivery verification, and cost notes.

document:supabase/docs/INVITE_GATE.md.name: INVITE_GATE.md
document:supabase/docs/INVITE_GATE.md.summary: Documents the invite-gate feature controlling closed-beta versus open registration, listing affected database functions and providing SQL instructions to toggle the gate on or off.

table:supabase/tests/admin_dashboard_sta.name: admin_dashboard_stats.sql
table:supabase/tests/admin_dashboard_sta.summary: pgTAP-style SQL test verifying that the public.admin_dashboard_stats() RPC is inaccessible to anon and non-admin roles, returns a non-null JSONB object for enabled admins, and includes required keys such as total_events, dead_letters, and generated_at.

table:supabase/tests/admin_db_health.sql.name: admin_db_health.sql
table:supabase/tests/admin_db_health.sql.summary: pgTAP-style SQL test verifying that public.admin_db_health_snapshot() is blocked for anon and non-admin roles, returns a valid JSONB object for enabled admins, and contains the expected keys including tag_queue_by_status and snapshot_at.

table:supabase/tests/admin_events_rpc.sq.name: admin_events_rpc.sql
table:supabase/tests/admin_events_rpc.sq.summary: Comprehensive SQL test for admin event RPCs (admin_events_enriched, admin_event_facets) verifying that non-admin, anon, and expired-admin callers are denied access while active admins can call these functions and receive correctly shaped results.

table:supabase/tests/api_surface_hardeni.name: api_surface_hardening.sql
table:supabase/tests/api_surface_hardeni.summary: Minimal SQL guard test asserting that pg_graphql is not installed and that the anon role holds no INSERT, UPDATE, DELETE, or other mutation grants on any public schema table.

table:supabase/tests/cron_rpc_security.s.name: cron_rpc_security.sql
table:supabase/tests/cron_rpc_security.s.summary: SQL test verifying that cron management RPCs (admin_list_railway_cron_jobs and related write functions) are restricted to enabled admins for reads and service_role for writes, blocking non-admin authenticated users and anon.

table:supabase/tests/cron_toggle.sql.name: cron_toggle.sql
table:supabase/tests/cron_toggle.sql.summary: SQL test verifying the cron enable/disable lifecycle: anon cannot call is_cron_enabled, service_role can, non-admin authenticated users cannot call admin_set_cron_enabled, and enabled admins can toggle a cron label off and back on.

table:supabase/tests/events_cursor_pagin.name: events_cursor_pagination.sql
table:supabase/tests/events_cursor_pagin.summary: SQL test covering seven cursor-pagination scenarios for events_enriched and search_events RPCs, including first/second page correctness, city filter, date range filter, same-datetime tie-breaking by UUID, anon path returning false for personalization fields, and keyword search.

table:supabase/tests/events_enriched_par.name: events_enriched_parity.sql
table:supabase/tests/events_enriched_par.summary: SQL test asserting behavioral parity of the events_enriched RPC across anon and authenticated paths, covering is_favorited/is_in_calendar flags, zero-rating vs rated aggregates, alphabetically ordered tags JSONB array, draft event exclusion, and city filter scoping.

table:supabase/tests/invite_gate_oauth_s.name: invite_gate_oauth_signup.sql
table:supabase/tests/invite_gate_oauth_s.summary: SQL test verifying the closed-beta invite gate blocks uninvited Google/Apple OAuth signups when app.settings.require_invite is enabled, while allowing pre-invited email addresses and passing through when the gate is disabled.

table:supabase/tests/llm_event_review_pr.name: llm_event_review_processing.sql
table:supabase/tests/llm_event_review_pr.summary: SQL test exercising the LLM event review pipeline: verifies default processing_mode is manual_review, tests manual/auto/LLM source processing modes, and validates that admin_events_enriched filters correctly by llm_review_status and llm_review_decision.

table:supabase/tests/reference_data.sql.name: reference_data.sql
table:supabase/tests/reference_data.sql.summary: SQL smoke test verifying that the production-reset migration baseline includes the expected seed event sources (BREC Parks, Louisiana Philharmonic, Macaroni Kid) and that removed legacy sources and required maintenance RPCs and cron jobs are present or absent as intended.

table:supabase/tests/rls_access_expiry.s.name: rls_access_expiry.sql
table:supabase/tests/rls_access_expiry.s.summary: SQL test verifying that access_expires_at is honored by is_enabled_user() and is_admin() helpers, blocking both regular users and admins whose access has expired even when is_enabled is true.

table:supabase/tests/rls_anon_read.sql.name: rls_anon_read.sql
table:supabase/tests/rls_anon_read.sql.summary: SQL test verifying the anonymous read boundary: anon can see published events via public_events view, can read published raw events rows, is blocked from draft events, can see active cities, and cannot see inactive cities.

table:supabase/tests/rls_privilege_escal.name: rls_privilege_escalation.sql
table:supabase/tests/rls_privilege_escal.summary: SQL test verifying three independent layers of defense against user_profiles.role privilege escalation: REVOKE on the role column, a trigger that blocks self-promotion, and the RLS WITH CHECK policy.

table:supabase/tests/rls_public_events_v.name: rls_public_events_view.sql
table:supabase/tests/rls_public_events_v.summary: SQL integration test for the RLS security boundary around public event previews, verifying anon visibility of published content, child-table (event_tags, ratings, comments) inheritance of the public_events boundary, and plan_events_for_user RPC user-ID enforcement.

table:supabase/tests/role_smoke.sql.name: role_smoke.sql
table:supabase/tests/role_smoke.sql.summary: Broad role smoke test covering 14 assertions across anon, authenticated, and service_role; verifies correct SELECT grants on public views and tables, correct RPC accessibility (events_enriched, search_events, is_cron_enabled), and that anon is blocked from cron RPCs.

table:supabase/tests/trace_retention.sql.name: trace_retention.sql
table:supabase/tests/trace_retention.sql.summary: SQL test verifying that private.run_daily_maintenance() prunes rows older than 90 days from event_ai_traces and source_extraction_traces while leaving recent rows intact.
